method_atom: a macroscopic complex order-parameter (coherent-phase) field governed by a nonlinear wave equation with interaction and, for driven-dissipative systems, gain and loss terms, whose phase winding around topological point defects is quantised in integer multiples so that circulation of the associated flow is discrete, together with amplitude-and-phase imaging to detect the spontaneous formation and pinning of such defects in the collectively ordered phase
object_atom: a fluid of exciton-polaritons — strongly interacting light-matter composite bosons in the strong-coupling regime of a semiconductor microcavity — in its Bose-condensed phase, whose superfluid character and quantised vortices are probed experimentally and modelled by a generalised Gross-Pitaevskii equation
brief_reason: the quantised-circulation topological-defect physics of a coherent nonlinear order-parameter field carries across any system, while the specific composite-boson polariton condensate in a microcavity is the condensed-matter-specific object.
